description: Failing to declare a dependency means CMake does not rebuild a target when an input changes, producing stale binaries.
body: Failure mode: a generated header or data file is consumed by a target but not declared as a dependency, so changing the input does not trigger recompilation. Detection signals: a source change has no effect until a clean rebuild; the binary embeds outdated content; incremental builds disagree with clean ones. Do not rely on build order alone for generated inputs. Safer fallback: declare the dependency explicitly via target sources, add_custom_command outputs, or add_dependencies so the build graph rebuilds the target when its real inputs change.
